description:
Authors: Han, C. and Wang, Z. and Zhao, H. and Ji, H.
Title: Explaining Emergent In-Context Learning as Kernel Regression
Year: 2023
URL: URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.12766
reason: The paper exists on arXiv with the exact title, authors, year, and URL provided. Semantic Scholar lists a near-identical paper with the same arXiv ID and authors but a slightly different title ('Understanding' vs 'Explaining'), which is a common metadata discrepancy. The core identifiers (arXiv:2305.12766, authors, year) match, confirming the paper is real.
prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.**

**Exact title:** Explaining Emergent In-Context Learning as Kernel Regression[1][2][6]

**Authors:** Chi Han, Ziqi Wang, Han Zhao, Heng Ji[2]

**Venue:** arXiv (preprint)[2]

**Year:** 2023 (submitted 22 May 2023, last revised 5 Oct 2023 for v2; v3 also available)[1][2]

**arXiv ID/DOI:** arXiv:2305.12766 (versions v1-v3)[1][2][6][8]

The paper proposes that large language models (LLMs) perform in-context learning (ICL) by simulating kernel regression, proving Bayesian inference on prompts asymptotically matches kernel regression form \(\hat{y} = \sum_i y_i K(x, x_i)/\sum_i K(x, x_i)\) as demonstrations increase. It includes empirical validation showing LLM attention and features align with kernel regression behaviors, explaining phenomena like sensitivity to similar samples, output formats, and in-distribution examples.[1][2][3][4] Code is available on GitHub.[6]
